content:
**Oklahoma City Thunder Capture First NBA Title in Franchise History**

June 23, 2025 — In a stunning conclusion to the 2024-2025 NBA season, the Oklahoma City Thunder have clinched their first NBA Championship, defeating the Boston Celtics in Game 6 of the Finals, 114–108, before a raucous sellout crowd at the Paycom Center.

Led by Finals MVP Shai Gilgeous-Alexander, who poured in 34 points and 8 assists in the clincher, the young Thunder squad completed their Cinderella run and fulfilled the high expectations set by their rapid rebuild. “We believed in each other every step of the way,” Gilgeous-Alexander said as confetti rained down. “This is for Oklahoma City, for our fans who have stayed with us through everything.”

Chet Holmgren, in his sophomore season, made his mark with 18 points, 12 rebounds, and 4 blocks, while Jalen Williams hit a clutch three-pointer with under a minute left to secure the victory. Head coach Mark Daigneault praised his team’s resilience: “We’ve grown together, we’ve fought together, and tonight we made history together.”

The Thunder’s path to the title included series victories over the defending champion Denver Nuggets, the Dallas Mavericks, and the top-seeded Minnesota Timberwolves. With this championship, Oklahoma City cements itself as the league’s new powerhouse and sets the stage for a new NBA era.
